title: ClaudeStudio Task
description: A ClaudeStudio Task: a packaged, parameterized, agent-driven workflow that the 6-tab Task Builder reads and writes. A .task.json file is the single source of truth for one task and is rendered into the builder UI, executed by the agentic Supervisor layer, and optionally scheduled.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this schema for editor tooling.

id.description: Stable, globally unique identifier. Lowercase kebab-case, used for storage keys, memory indexing and deep links.

name.description: Human-readable task name shown in the library and run history.

description.description: One or two sentence summary shown as a subtitle in the library card and the Builder 'Overview' tab.

icon.description: SF Symbols name (preferred on macOS) or an emoji. Rendered on the library card.

category.description: Primary grouping used for the library sidebar.

tags.description: Free-form searchable tags used by the library filter and by semantic memory.

scope.description: Visibility / availability of the task. 'project' tasks live in the project's .claudestudio dir; 'user' tasks are personal and available across projects; 'global' tasks ship with the app or an installed pack.

version.description: Semantic version of the task definition, for community packs and update detection.

author.description: Optional author or maintainer attribution for community-contributed tasks.

agent.description: Agent execution envelope. Maps to the Builder 'Agent' tab and constrains how the Supervisor runs the workflow.

model.description: Default Claude model id for the run. Individual steps may override.

allowed_tools.description: Allow-list of tools the agent may use. Maps directly to Claude Code permission settings. Empty array means no tools (pure reasoning).

budget_usd.description: Soft spend cap in USD for a single run. The Supervisor pauses and asks for confirmation when exceeded. Omit for no cap.

isolation.description: Execution environment. 'worktree' runs in a dedicated git worktree; 'sandbox' runs read-only/no-network; 'inplace' runs directly in the working directory.

inputs.description: Parameters collected before a run. Each input is rendered as a form field in the Builder 'Inputs' tab and the run dialog. Values are substituted into workflow prompts as {{key}}.

key.description: Placeholder key. Referenced in prompts as {{key}}.

label.description: Field label shown in the form.

help.description: Optional helper text shown below the field.

required.description: Whether the run dialog blocks until a value is provided. Defaults to false.

default.description: Default value, type must match the field 'type'.

workflow.description: The ordered chain of agent steps. Maps to the Builder 'Workflow' tab.

steps.description: Ordered steps. Each step is a single agent turn whose prompt may reference {{input}} placeholders and outputs of prior steps.

id.description: Optional step id, referenced by later steps.

title.description: Short label for the step shown in the run timeline.

prompt.description: Instruction sent to the agent for this step. May contain {{key}} placeholders.

tools.description: Optional per-step tool allow-list. Must be a subset of agent.allowed_tools.

output.description: What the task produces and what happens afterwards. Maps to the Builder 'Output' tab.

type.description: Primary artifact type produced by the final step.

path.description: Optional output path template (may contain {{key}} placeholders).

post_hook.description: Optional shell command or Claude Code hook id run after the artifact is produced (e.g. open the file, run a formatter, post a notification).

schedule.description: Optional automation. When present, the task can be registered with the cron/trigger system. Maps to the Builder 'Schedule' tab.

trigger.description: How the task fires. 'cron' uses a cron expression; 'on_event' subscribes to an Event-Bus topic; 'manual' is run-on-demand only.

cron.description: Standard 5-field cron expression. Required when trigger is 'cron'.

event.description: Event-Bus topic to subscribe to (e.g. 'git.post_commit', 'file.changed'). Required when trigger is 'on_event'.

enabled.description: Whether the schedule is currently active.
